Anything to be said or done clandestinely or in secret; a secret or underhand business or work to be going on.

more: s. m. 1. Unripe grapes or dates. 2. A colour in pigeons.

s. m. A fly boat ( Harris ).

more: s. m. Name of a medicine. (Menispermum glabrum).

ety: inf. n. of غرب 'to become a stranger or foreigner, to go to a foreign country'
def: s.f. Travelling (to foreign countries), going abroad; emigration; — being far from (one's) home or native country; the state or condition of a stranger, or foreigner, or exile; wretchedness, misery; humility, lowliness
more: s. f. 1. Travelling, emigration, being far from oneʼs countrymen and friends. 2. Wretchedness. غربت زده g̠ẖurbat-zada, Oppressed with wretchedness.
subs: adj. Oppressed with misery or wretchedness, wretched, miserable.

def: s.m. See s.v. گرو guru. adj. & s.m. (fem. gurvi, or gurviṇī), Heavy, weighty; great, large, extended, long (as a vowel), grave (as an accent); difficult, hard; high (in degree), vehement, violent, excessive, deep, much; important, serious, momentous; valuable, highly prized, dear, beloved; venerable, honourable, respectable; excellent; best; — one who is an object of reverence, a venerable or respectable person (as a father, mother, &c.); a spiritual parent or preceptor (from whom a youth receives the initiatory Mantra or prayer and who conducts the necessary ceremonies up to the period of investiture with the sacrificial thread); a spiritual guide, a religious teacher (one who explains the law and religion to his pupil, instructs him in the Śāstras, &c.); a pastor; a teacher (generally); a superior; — a name of Brihaspati or the planet Jupiter (who is considered as the guru or preceptor of the gods); — a wise man, a sage ( = gur-gyānī); — (ironic.) a consummate knave
subs: vulg. gur-bhāʼī, s.m. Follower of the same spiritual preceptor or guide, fellow-sectarian, fellow-disciple; fellow-student
s.m. (f. -ī), The child (natural or adopted) of a religious preceptor

vulg. gur-jan, s.m. pl. Religious or venerable persons, eminent persons, great people, respectable people; — s.m. sing. A venerable person (as a father, a religious preceptor, &c.), an elder, a superior
vulg. gur-daććhanā, s.f. A fee given to a spiritual preceptor; rent-free land given to a religious teacher
s.m. The pronouncing a blessing on a religious guide
s.m. A respectful epithet of any guru
s.m. The eighth Nakshatra or lunar constellation Pushya (of which Brihaspati is the ruling deity)
s.m. A serious or momentous affair; — the business or office of a spiritual teacher
s.m. 'A guru with bells' (hung round his neck), a thorough knave
vulg. guru-laćh, or gur-laćh, s.m. = next
adj. (f. -ā), Heavy, hard of digestion; — s.m. Knowledge which can be acquired from a living instructor only
s.f. The wife of a spiritual teacher
(or gur-) mukh honā, v.n. To receive the initiatory Mantra from a guru; to become a disciple or pupil
s.f. Name of the character in which the Panjābī language is written
vulg. gur-bār, s.m. 'The day of Jupiter,' Thursday.

P.; n. m. S. गुडची. A medicine; Menispermum glabrum or cordifolium , Wat. The infusion is used as a febrifuge.
s. f. Name of a creeping plant. (Menispermum glabrum).

gurdwara

ety: rt. of gureḵẖtan = prep. gu = Zend vi = S. वि+reḵẖtan (rt. rez), fr. Zend rt. rić = S. रिच्
def: part. adj. Flying, fleeing, fugitive (used as last member of compounds); — s.f. Flying (from); flight, escape; avoiding, shunning; a diversion, digression; deviation; regression; abstaining (from), abstinence; fasting; aversion, dislike, abhorrence
more: n. f. گريختن to fly. 1. Escape flight. 2. ( Rhet .) A diversion; digression. gurez k. , v. a. To shun; avoid; evade. insāf se gurez k. , v. a. To evade justice.

(from گريختن) 1. s. f. Flight, deviation, digression, abhorrence, aversion, dislike, abstinence, fasting, regression. 2. part. act. (in compos.) Fugitive, fleeing. گريزپا gurez-pā, adj. Fugitive, runaway, fleeing.
subs: (-se), To fly (from); to avoid, shun; to evade (e.g. inṣāf-se gurez karnā); — to abstain (from); to be averse (to), &c.
s.f. Time or place of fleeing; the place (or occasion) in which it is proper to avoid or fly (from a person or thing); place of escape; place to fly to, place of refuge, asylum, retreat.

ety: imperf. part. of gureḵẖtan; see gurez
def: part. Taking flight, fleeing, fugitive.

s. f. Ague, the cold fit of a fever, a quotidian fever.

n. f. S. कुरूण्टी 1. A doll. 1. Damṛī kī guṛyā, ṭakā ḍolī kā! Prov. For the doll but a farthing you pay, and a penny to fetch her away! 2. Shādī hai, kuchh guṛyoṅ kā byāh thoṛā hī hai! Prov. Saying. A marriage feast is not a doll's wedding. (It is an expensive affair). 3. Tulsī! pratimā pūjibo jiyoṅ guṛyoṅ kā khel, Bheṭ bhai jab pīū se dhare piṭārī mel . Tulsī Dās. Tulsī! idol-worship is doll's play, Found a husband dolls are put away. 2. A humble or poor bride. Guṛyā saṅvār dūṅgī, arī, bhīk māṅg kar . Wom. Jān Sāhab. guṛyoṅ kā ālā , m. ( lit . niche). A doll's house; a baby house. guṛyoṅ kā biyāh , n. m. 1. Marriage between two dolls. 2. A poor man's wedding. guṛyoṅ kā khel , n. m. 1. Playing with dolls. 2. An easy matter.
s. f. A bead (of a rosary, &c.).

Kabira [encore], Yeh Jawāni Hai Dīwānī
... گُڑیا رے گُڑیا تیرا گُڈّا پَردیسِیا جوڑی آسمنی ہوگئی شَگُن پے دیکھو شادمانی ہوگئی ...
... guṛyā re guṛyā terā guḍḍā pardesiyā joṛī āsmnī hogī śagun pe dekho śādmānī hogī ...
Oh doll oh doll, your male-doll is a foreigner. Your match/pairing has become heavenly. On the omen, look happiness has become.

ety: for A. غرة g̠urrat, inf. n. of غرّ 'to have whiteness, or a white patch, &c. on the forehead' (a horse
def: s.m. Whiteness; clearnness, brightness; — a white mark, a star, or blaze on the forehead of a horse; — a speckled, or iron-grey, or roan horse; — the first, or commencement (of the month, &c.), the night in which the new moon is seen, the first day of the moon, calends; — the first appearance or dawn of day, morning or its splendour; — the face, aspect, forehead, front (of a man); — the first, the best, or the chiefest (of anything); — g̠urra batānā, or g̠urra denā (-ko), To put (one) off to the (next) new moon; to put off, to shuffle with, to get rid of, &c.; to pass over or by (syn. ṭāl-denā).

ety: g̠ur˚, prob. = S. गर् (गॄ); but cf. S. घुर् cf. also P. g̠urrīdan, whence g̠urrish: — rt. Zend g̠ar = S. गर्
def: v.n. To growl; to roar; to purr (as a cat); to frown, look menacing.

def: s.f. = گڙسي gursī, q.v. s.f. An earthen vessel or bowl (such as a goldsmith uses for his fire); fire-pan, brasier; fire-place, grate (cf. bursī); — (dialec.) cot, cottage, cabin, hut.

adj. & s.m. (fem. gurvi, or gurviṇī), Heavy, weighty; great, large, extended, long (as a vowel), grave (as an accent); difficult, hard; high (in degree), vehement, violent, excessive, deep, much; important, serious, momentous; valuable, highly prized, dear, beloved; venerable, honourable, respectable; excellent; best; — one who is an object of reverence, a venerable or respectable person (as a father, mother, &c.); a spiritual parent or preceptor (from whom a youth receives the initiatory Mantra or prayer and who conducts the necessary ceremonies up to the period of investiture with the sacrificial thread); a spiritual guide, a religious teacher (one who explains the law and religion to his pupil, instructs him in the Śāstras, &c.); a pastor; a teacher (generally); a superior; — a name of Brihaspati or the planet Jupiter (who is considered as the guru or preceptor of the gods); — a wise man, a sage ( = gur-gyānī); — (ironic.) a consummate knave
more: n. m. guryānī , n. f. 1. A religious teacher; spiritual guide. 1. Gur gur hī rahe, chele chīnī ho gaë . Prov. The priest treacle (a priest) remains, his pupil sugar's rank attains (high secular office). 2. Gurū sharan gati āë-ke jo sumre Siyā Rām, Yahāṅ rahe ānaṅd se, aṅt base Haridhām . Who at priest's feet cry 'Sītā Ram' continually, Joy's theirs where'er they be, their god reached finally. 3. Pūjā gur kī kījye, sab pūjā jeṅhi māṅhi, Jab jal sīṅche mūl meṅ, sakhā, patr aghāiṅ . The worship of your priest all worship comprehends, As water at the root to all the leaves extends. 4. Gur bin mile na gyān, bhāg bin mile na sampat . Prov. Without a teacher knowledge you cant get, without good luck no riches you can get. 2. A teacher. Gurū bin byākul chelvā, kaṅṭh bin bāur gīt . Bhoj. Prov. Disciple restless priest without, as song sounds harsh good voice without. 3. ( gur gyānī ) A wise man; …
1. adj. Important, weighty, honourable, respectable. 2. s. m. A spiritual guide, tutor, pastor, teacher; the planet Jupiter. گرومکهہ هونا guru-mukh honā, To become a scholar.
subs: vulg. gur-bhāʼī, s.m. Follower of the same spiritual preceptor or guide, fellow-sectarian, fellow-disciple; fellow-student
s.m. (f. -ī), The child (natural or adopted) of a religious preceptor

vulg. gur-jan, s.m. pl. Religious or venerable persons, eminent persons, great people, respectable people; — s.m. sing. A venerable person (as a father, a religious preceptor, &c.), an elder, a superior
vulg. gur-daććhanā, s.f. A fee given to a spiritual preceptor; rent-free land given to a religious teacher
s.m. The pronouncing a blessing on a religious guide
s.m. A respectful epithet of any guru
s.m. The eighth Nakshatra or lunar constellation Pushya (of which Brihaspati is the ruling deity)
s.m. A serious or momentous affair; — the business or office of a spiritual teacher
s.m. 'A guru with bells' (hung round his neck), a thorough knave
vulg. guru-laćh, or gur-laćh, s.m. = next
adj. (f. -ā), Heavy, hard of digestion; — s.m. Knowledge which can be acquired from a living instructor only
s.f. The wife of a spiritual teacher
(or gur-) mukh honā, v.n. To receive the initiatory Mantra from a guru; to become a disciple or pupil
s.f. Name of the character in which the Panjābī language is written
vulg. gur-bār, s.m. 'The day of Jupiter,' Thursday.
wety: From Sanskrit गुरु (guru).

def: s.m. = guru-laksh, q.v.s.v. guru.
more: 1. s. m. A saint, a holy man, a spiritual director. گورو لچهہ gūrū-lachh ( गुरुलक्ष्य ) Knowledge which can be acquired from a living instructor only. 2. adj. Heavy, hard of digestion.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit गौरव (gaurava, “cumbrousness, importance”); see गौर (gaura, “shining, brilliant”) for more.

def: s.f. The office, or profession, or condition of a guru or spiritual preceptor.
s.f. The wife of a spiritual preceptor; a religious preceptress.
s.f. = guruʼāʼin, q.v. s.f. The wife of a spiritual preceptor; a religious preceptress.

ety: inf. n. of غرب 'to be hidden'; 'to set,' &c.
def: s.m. Setting (of the sun, &c.); occultation; — sunset; — the west
more: n. m. 1. Setting (of the sun, etc.); occultation (H. ast , 1). 2. Sunset.
s. m. 1. Setting (of the sun, &c.). 2. The west. غروب هونا g̠ẖurūb honā, To set (the sun or moon, &c.).
subs: v.n. To set (the sun, or moon, &c.).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian غروب (ġurūb), from Arabic غُرُوب (ḡurūb).

def: s. m. Thursday.
s.m. (orig.) 'A thing by which one is deceived'; pride, haughtiness, vanity, vainglory
more: n. m. Pride (ابهمان). Tagāful, hayā, nāz, shokhī, gurūr, Har ek apne mauqe se vaqt-i-zurūr . Mīr Hasan. Indifferent, modest, loving, saucy, proud, Each pretty air upon occasion showed. gurūr k. , v. a. To be proud ( dimāg k .).
(from غرّ) s. m. Pride, haughtiness, vain glory, vanity.
subs: v.n. To practise haughtiness, to be proud, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Persian غرور, from Arabic غُرُور (ḡurūr).

def: s.f. = g̠urūr.
v.n. (dialec.) To cry, stopping the breath; to sob.
more: v. a. S. क्रुश. To sob. (?)

adj. & s.f. Heavy (with child), pregnant; — a pregnant woman; — the wife of a guru or teacher.
adj. & s.f. Heavy (with child), pregnant; — a pregnant woman ( = garbhinī).

ety: Pehl. vazr; Zend vazra, fr. vaz, 'To strengthen,' &c.; S. वज्र, rt. वज्
def: s.m. A mace, a club; an iron club (pointed at one end, and having a knob at the other covered with spikes); — a battle-axe
more: n. m. An iron club; a mace. gurz-bardār , n. m. A mace-bearer. gurz-mār , n. m. A Mah. Faqīr, who carries a club armed with spikes with which he wounds himself to extort alms.
s. m. A mace, a sort of iron club pointed at one end and having a knob at the other covered with spikes; a battle-axe. گرز بردار gurz-bardār, A mace-bearer.
subs: s.m. A (Mohammadan) faqīr who carries a club armed with spikes with which he wounds himself to extort alms.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گُرْز (gurz). In the Indo-Persian tradition, it became associated with lightning-bolts, thunder and storms. It became a metaphor for strength and force as well, especially in relation to the warrior and fighter.

def: s.m. A bound, jump.

s.m. 'The master or possessor of cows or of herds'; an epithet of the Deity; — a religious mendicant (a faqīr), a devotee, a saint or holy man; — a caste of Brāhmaṇs, descendants of the disciples of Ćaitanya of Nadiyā; a Brāhmaṇ of this class; — an honorary title affixed to proper names.

ety: g̠uṣṣa, q.v. + Prk. इल्लो and इल्लओ = S. इलः and इल+कः
def: adj. Angry, wrathful, passionate, irascible, choleric, furious, fierce.

n. m. 1. See سائيں Gusāīṅ te gadhā bhalā jo dhobī ghare lādī ḍhove . Bhoj. Prov. More useful than the priest is the laundress' donkey who bears a load to the house. 2. A saint; a holy man; a devotee; priest. Jo ye bachche mere parvān chaṛh jāëṅ to maiṅ jānūṅ, Gusāīṅ jī! raṅḍāpe meṅ bharosā hai Gusaiyāṅ kā . If but this child grow up to wed God's grace I'll own, O priest! a widow I, my trust's in God alone. 3. A caste of Brahmans. 4. A title of respect.

s.m. 'The master or possessor of cows or of herds'; an epithet of the Deity; — a religious mendicant (a faqīr), a devotee, a saint or holy man; — a caste of Brāhmaṇs, descendants of the disciples of Ćaitanya of Nadiyā; a Brāhmaṇ of this class; — an honorary title affixed to proper names.

def: s.m. Water with which a thing has been washed; — bathing.
more: s. m. 1. Dirty water with which any thing has been washed. 2. Bathing.

def: s.m. = next, q.v.

more: part. act. (from گساردن) in compos. Letting go, taking away, drinking or dissipating (wine, grief).

def: part. adj. Breaking, tearing; destroying; removing (used as last member of compounds; e.g. gam-gusil, 'destroying or removing care').

ety: g̠uṣṣa, q.v. + Prk. इल्लो and इल्लओ = S. इलः and इल+कः
def: adj. Angry, wrathful, passionate, irascible, choleric, furious, fierce.
more: A. P. adj. Irascible; choleric; passionate.

r: gusista, gusistan, gusīḵẖtan, gu, vi, sistan, sīḵẖtan, śar
ety: perf. part. of gusistan = gusīḵẖtan; gu = Zend vi = S. वि; sistan or sīḵẖtan, fr. Zend rt. śar = S. शर् (श्टनाति
def: part. adj. Broken; interrupted; abrupt.
s.f. Breaking; breach, fracture; interruption.
s.m. A complete (purificatory) washing of the whole person; bathing, ablution, a bath
more: n. m. 1. Bathing; a bath. 2. ( Mah .) Purifying by frequent immersion in water. gusal-khānā , n. m. A bath-room; bagnio. gusl-i-sehat , n. m. Bathing after recovering from sickness. gusal k. , v. n. To bathe. gusul kī hājat honā , v. n. To be polluted. gusal, yā gusl-i-maiyat denā , v. a. To wash a corpse.
s. m. Bathing, ablution. غسل خانه g̠ẖusl-ḵẖāna, s. m. A bath, bagnio.
subs: (-ko), To wash, bathe; to wash a corpse ( = g̠usl-ě-maiyit denā)
or g̠usl-ě-siḥḥat, s.m. Bathing after recovery from sickness
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian غسل (ġusl), from Arabic غُسْل (ḡusl).

def: bath, bathroom (for bathing)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian غسل خانه (ġusl-xāna), the first element from Arabic غُسْل (ḡusl). Compare Ottoman Turkish غسلخانه (gusülhane, “bathroom”).

def: alternative form of غُسْل خانَہ (ġusl xāna)

ety: for A. غصة, v.n. fr. غصّ 'to be choked,' &c.
def: s.m. Choking, strangulation, suffocation; — (choking) wrath, rage, anger, passion; — grief, disquietude of mind, anxiety; — g̠uṣṣe, adv. In anger; from or through anger
more: n. m. غص his throat choked. Rage ( tezī , 6). 1. Gussā harām hai . Prov. Anger is a bastard (or unlawfull). 2. Gusse meṅ bhalāī burāī nahīṅ sūjhtī . Prov. Anger knows not good from bad. 3. Zarā gussā ā jāë, dekho! kaisā pīṭtā hūṅ! Wait till I am the least bit angry, and see how I'll beat you! 4. Gusse meṅ aql jātī rahtī hai . Anger is an enemy to reason. 5. Zor thoṛā, gussa bahut, mār khāne kī nishānī . Prov. Weak and angry, punishment sure. 6. Inkī nāk par gussā rakkhā hī rahtā hai . Prov. His nose is alwsys red with passion. gussā pīnā, mārnā, yā thūknā , Tir. gussa ghoṅṭnā , v. n. To suppress one's anger. 1. Ham to pī jāte lahū dushman kā, par kuchh sochkar Zafar. Dil meṅ apne gussā apnā, aë sitam-gar, pī gaë . I would have drunk my rival's blood but then I thought a while And in my heart my rage suppressed, O cruel fair one, smile! 2. Chalo, jāne do! gussā thūk do! gussā dilānā , v. …

(from غصّ g̠ẖaṣṣa, To be choked or suffocated) s. m. 1. Strangulation, suffocation. 2. ( met. ) Anxiety, grief. 3. (in Hindūst.) Anger, passion. غصي g̠ẖuṣṣe, In anger. غصي سي بهوت هوجانا g̠ẖuṣṣe se bhūt hojānā, To rage like a fiend. غصه کرنا g̠ẖuṣṣa karnā, To rage, to be in a passion. غصه ناک g̠ẖuṣṣa-nāk, adj. Passionate. غصه ور g̠ẖuṣṣa-war, adj. Passionate, cholerick.

def: adj. Presumptuous, arrogant, insolent, audacious, impudent, saucy, uncivil, rude; cruel; abrupt.
more: adj. Impudent; audacious (بےادب 2). gustākhā'nah , adj. Presumptuously; arrogantly; rudely.
adj. Presumptuous, arrogant, insolent, saucy, uncivil, cruel, rude, abrupt. گستاخانه gustāḵẖ-āna, adv. Presumptuously, arrogantly: adj. see گستاخ.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گُسْتاخ.

def: adv. Presumptuously, arrogantly, rudely, &c. (see gustāḵẖ).
s.f. Presumption, arrogance, insolence, audacity, assurance, sauciness, rudeness; contempt (of court); cruelty
more: n. f. See بےادبي 1. Presumption; audacity. 2. Contempt (of court). gustākhī k. , v. a. To be insolent; to presume. gustākhī muāf , intj. Pardon my rudeness!
s. f. Presumption, arrogance, insolence, sauciness, assurance, rudeness, cruelty. گستاخي کرني gustāḵẖī-karnī, To presume, to be arrogant, &c.
subs: To presume, to be arrogant, or insolent, or rude, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گُسْتَاخِی (gustāxī). By surface analysis, گُسْتَاخ (gustāx) + ـی (-ī).

more: part. act. (from گستردن) in compos. Spreading, strewing, scattering, diffusing; spreader, dispenser, administrator.

def: part. Spread (as a bed, &c.); having spread; — s.m. That which is spread; a foundation.

v.t. To swallow, gulp, bolt; — v.n. To coo (as a dove, &c.)
more: v. n. To coo as a dove. 2. To swallow.

def: v. n. To gulp down. Chār khāīṅ chaṭrā kī, chār khāīṅ maṭrā kī, Sorah guṭak gaī gehuan kī! Wom. song. Four cakes of gram he ate, and four of peas, and sixteen wheaten cakes gulped down!
v.n. To coo.
v.t. To thread, string; to entangle; to entwine; to embarrass; to engage in; — to fix; to contrive.
more: v. a. Caus. of گتنا q. v.

s.m. A kind of prickly plant, Solanum melongena (syn. bhaṭ-kaṭaiyā).
n. m. A prickly plant (بهتَکتَيا); solanum melongena , Wat.

ety: Prk. गुत्थं; S. ग्रथितं; or v.n. of gūthnā, q.v.
def: s.m. A round ball (of cotton, &c.); a string or thread (of anything).
more: s. m. A round ball of cotton, &c.

def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Blunted; set on edge (the teeth).
s.m. A large lump; a large stone (of fruit), &c. ( = guṭṭhal, and guṭhlī).
more: adj. Being set on edge (the teeth).

def: v.n. To be dulled or blunted; to be set on edge (the teeth).

s.f. A knot; a lump, mass (as of curd in milk, or of flour in broth, &c.), a clot (of blood); a ball, lump, gathering (as of cotton in a quilt, &c.); — kernel; stone (of a fruit); — a gland; a glandular swelling; a bump; a hard boil or tumour; hard lump (in a woman's breast); hard part (of a boil, &c.); a lump of hardened fæces.
more: s. f. A kernel, stone (of fruit, &c.), seed.

def: n. f. See āṅṭhī. Ber guṭhlī sab' hazam . Prov. Saying. Pulp and seed both swallowed. (Undiscerning).

ety: gūth˚ = Prk. गुत्थो = S. ग्रथितः, rt. ग्रथ्
def: v.t. To thread, string; to plait, braid; to stitch; to put on a spit or skewer, to spit ( = gūṅthnā).
more: v. a. 1. To string; thread. 2. To plait; braid.
( गुंफन or caus. of گتهنا) v. a. 1. To plait, to thread, to string. 2. To stitch. 3. To spit, or put on a spit.

ety: gut˚ or guth˚ = Prk. गुत्थ(इ) = S. ग्रथ्य(ते), pass. of rt. ग्रथ्
def: v.n. To be strung, to be threaded; to be interlaced, or intertwined, or intermingled; to be braided, be plaited; to be or become tangled (as thread, &c.), to be ravelled; to tangle; to catch, hitch; to be entangled (in); to be embarrassed, be involved in difficulties; — to close (with, -se, an antagonist, in wrestling, syn. juṭnā); — to be taken up (with), be occupied, or engaged (in), be absorbed (in); to be under employment, or in use
more: v. a. S. गुफ arrange, string. 1. To be threaded; strung; braided; plaited. 2. To wrestle or close with ( juṭnā 3). 3. To be taken up with; absorbed. Khiyāl meṅ guthā huā hai .
v. n. To be plaited, to be threaded, to be strung.
subs: (dialec.), v.t. To settle, fix, determine, contrive ( = gutānā).

ety: Prk. गुत्थओ (with w inserted); S. ग्रथित+कः
def: adj. (f. -wīṅ), Strung together; interlaced, intertwined; plaited, &c.
adj. Plaited or strung together. Guthvāṅ ḍorā .
v.t. = guṅthwānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to string, &c.; — to cause to be strung; to have or get plaited, &c. (see gūṅthnā).
s.f. A pill, bolus; a small globe or ball, a bullet, a marble, a pellet; a pearl.

s.m. (dialec.) = guttā, q.v. s.m. (dialec.) Exclusive right (of sale, supply, &c.); monopoly, contract; — an income of variable amount sold (or let) for a fixed sum; lease (of land, &c.), farm (syn. ijāra).
more: n. m. S. गुटिका 1. A magic ball prepared by devotees, by putting which in their mouths they are believed to become invisible. 2. A small book worn as an amulet. 3. A hand book; a manual. 4. A sweetmeat swallowed as a bolus without chewing. Chāṭ hai dahī ke guṭke kī! Delhī street cry. 5. A folded betel leaf with catechu, lime, etc. 6. Shards used in play.

def: s.m. A small ball, a bolus; a magic ball prepared by devotees (which, if put into the mouth, is supposed to make a person invisible, or to accomplish something wonderful); — a sweetmeat swallowed as a bolus without chewing; — a small book worn as an amulet; — a little book (bound or boarded); a handbook, a manual; — a flake of pān or betel-leaf (syn. khīlī); a mouthful; a gulp; — a round pebble, a shard (used in play; cf. giṭkarī, and gittī).
more: s. m. 1. A ball prepared by devotees, by putting which in their mouths they are supposed to become invisible. 2. A sweetmeat swallowed as a bolus without chewing. 3. A small book worn as an amulet.

ety: guṭkā or giṭkā, q.v. + S. इनी
def: adj. & s.f. = guṭṭī, fem. of guṭṭā, q.v.
( Mech .) Cor. of dog chuck.
s.m. = H گٿلي गुटली guṭlī, s.f. dialec. forms of guṭhlā, and guṭhlī, qq.v.
s.f. = H گٿلا गुटला guṭlā, s.m. dialec. forms of guṭhlā, and guṭhlī, qq.v.
more: s. (dakh.) Stone (of fruit, Harris ): see گٿهلي guṭhlī.

ety: gut˚ or guth˚ = Prk. गुत्थ(इ) = S. ग्रथ्य(ते), pass. of rt. ग्रथ्
def: v.n. To be strung, to be threaded; to be interlaced, or intertwined, or intermingled; to be braided, be plaited; to be or become tangled (as thread, &c.), to be ravelled; to tangle; to catch, hitch; to be entangled (in); to be embarrassed, be involved in difficulties; — to close (with, -se, an antagonist, in wrestling, syn. juṭnā); — to be taken up (with), be occupied, or engaged (in), be absorbed (in); to be under employment, or in use
more: 1. v. n. (dakh. i. q. مشغول هونا) To be occupied or busied (?). 2. v. a. (i. q. مقرر کرنا) To settle, to fix, to establish (?).
subs: (dialec.), v.t. To settle, fix, determine, contrive ( = gutānā).

def: s.m. (dialec.) Exclusive right (of sale, supply, &c.); monopoly, contract; — an income of variable amount sold (or let) for a fixed sum; lease (of land, &c.), farm (syn. ijāra).
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Short of stature, stumpy, stunted; — a stumpy person, a dwarf.

ety: prob. Prk. गुत्थलो or गुट्ठलो(?); S. ग्रथ्+अलः
def: adj. & s.m. Having knots, knotted; lumpy, bumpy; coagulated; hardened; having a large stone (as a mango, &c.); — a knot; a hard lump; a large bump; coagulation; — a gland; a glandular swelling; a hard boil or tumour (cf. guṭhlī).
n. m. 1. Coagulation ( āṅṭhī , 1). 2. A hard lump; a knot. Razaī meṅ guṭṭhal paṛ gaë . 3. A gland ( gānṭh ). guṭ'ṭhal , adj. Having a large stone (a fruit). Guṭṭhal ām .
s.f. Betel-nut, the nut of Areca catechu.
s.m. A witness, an evidence; testimony, proof
more: n. m. pl. گواﮩـان A witness. gavāh banānā, yā ṭhairānā , v. a. To name a person as a witness. gavāh-i-tālīmī, sikhāyā yā paṛhāyā gavāh , A tutored witness. gavāh-i-hāshiyah, yā mundarjā dastāvez , A marginal witness; an attesting witness. gavāh denā , v. a. To produce a witness. gavāh-i-rūyat, gavāh-i-chashm-dīd, dinī, yā mashāhdā , An eye-witness ( sākshī , 3). gavāh-i-samāī , n. m. A hearsay witness. gavāh-i-aqd-i-nikāh , n. A witness to a marriage contract. gavāh k. , v. a. To make one a witness. gavāh ko paṛhānā, yā sikhānā . To instruct a witness. gavāh muddaī , A witness for the prosecution. gavāh-i-muddāilah , n. m. A witness for the defence. gavāhoṅ kī ism-navīsī . A list of witnesses.
s. m. A witness, an evidence.
subs: or gawāh ṭhaharānā (-ko), To make (one) a witness, to name (a person) as a witness
or gawāh karnā (-ko), To bring forward (or to produce) evidence or a witness or witnesses; to make (one) a witness
(-ko), To bring evidence; to take a witness; to invoke
(-par), To become a witness; to bear testimony (to).
wety: From Classical Persian گواه (guwāh).

def: s.f. Evidence, witness, testimony; deposition; written testimony
more: s. f. Evidence, testimony, witness. گواهي دينا gawāhī denā, To witness, testify.
subs: (-par), To give evidence, to witness (to), bear testimony (to), testify, attest; to depose (to)
or gawāhī likhnā (-par), To witness (a document), to attest.

ety: P. go+waiyā, as in the preceding
def: adj. Conversible; eloquent; — s.f. A gossip; a (woman's) female friend, &c. ( = guʼiyāṅ, q.v.).
more: s. f. (gossip) A womanʼs female friend. adj. Eloquent, conversible.

def: s.m. The betel-nut tree, Areca faufel or catechu.

ety: rt. of guwāshtan, or guwārīdan; gu = Zend vi = S. वि+wār, fr. Pehl. var = Zend var = S. वर् (वृ)
def: part. adj. Digestible; palatable, &c. (see guwārā; — it is used as last member of compounds, e.g. ḵẖẉush-gawār, and nā-gawār, q.v.s.v. ḵẖẉush and nā).
more: (vulg. gawār ) adj. 1. Palatable, digestible. 2. Agreeable, pleasant. گوارا کرنا gawārā karnā, To bear, endure, submit to, to take (upon oneʼs self trouble, &c.).

def: adj. = gawārā, q.v. s.m. A swing; a cradle.
more: adj. Palatable; agreeable. gavārah k. , v. n. To bear; endure; submit to. khush-gavār , adj. Tasty; savoury; nice. nā-gavār , adj. Disagreeable; unpleasant; offensive.
(or gawāra ) adj. 1. Palatable, digestible. 2. Agreeable, pleasant. گوارا کرنا gawārā karnā, To bear, endure, submit to, to take (upon oneʼs self trouble, &c.).

ety: imperf. part. of guwārīdan; see guwār
def: part. adj. Digesting; stomaching, swallowing, putting up with; — digestible; palatable; agreeable, pleasant
more: (or gawārā ) adj. 1. Palatable, digestible. 2. Agreeable, pleasant. گوارا کرنا gawārā karnā, To bear, endure, submit to, to take (upon oneʼs self trouble, &c.).
subs: v.t. To make (a thing) palatable (to oneself, apne-ūpar); to take (upon oneself); to put up with, to submit to, to stomach, to swallow, to endure or bear.

def: s.f. An electuary (or a compound) for assisting digestion.
s.m. A fart
subs: (-ko), 'To regard as a camel's fart'; to disregard, to treat lightly.

ety: prob. akin to S. गोदः or गोर्दः; H. gūdā
def: s.m. A nut; a walnut; a kernel (cf. ćil-goza).
more: n. m. A fart ( pād ). goz uṛānā, yā mārnā , v. n. To fart; pass wind ( pādnā ). goz-i-shutar samajhnā , v. n. To treat lightly; disregard. Merī bāt ko goz-i-shutar samajhtā hai. baiṭhe goz mārnā, Slang , v. n. To sit idle; to do no work.
s. m. f. A fart. ¶ gauz or gūz, A walnut. ¶ gawaz, contract. of گوزن gawazn, q. v.

def: s.f. An inconsiderate word, a vain or rash speech; boasting; vanity; — a falsehood, a lie
more: s. f. A falsehood, a lie, an inconsiderate word, vain or rash speech. See گزاف.

ety: imperf. part. of guzīdan = prep. gu+zīdan = ćīdan; Zend vi+rt. ći; S. वि+चि (चिनोति)
def: part. adj. Choosing, selecting; adopting (see guzīn).

ety: rt. of guẕārdan or guẕāshtan, causat. of guẕashtan; gu˚ = Zend vi = S. वि; — zash˚, fr. Zend rt. tar = S. तर्(तॄ)
def: s.m. A passing; passage, transit; a ferry (cf. guẕāra, and guẕar).
more: s. m. 1. A ferry, a passage. 2. part. (from گذاريدن) in compos. Passing, causing to pass. 3. Performing, executing, paying.

def: to pass by; to pass away
(For this and other words beginning with guz˚ which are not to be found below, see under گذ guẓ˚.)
more: ( In comp .) Paying; executing, as māl-guzār a rent-payer or tenant.

def: s.m. A passing by; ingress and egress; a passage; a path, way, road; a pass; a ford; a ferry; a toll-bar; — escape; — jurisdiction; — living, life, &c. ( = guẕāra, guẕrān); — adj. Passing, elapsing
more: 1. s. m. A passage, a ferry, ford. 2. adj. Passing, elapsing.
subs: s.m. A ford; a canal; an aqueduct
s.m. A ferry-man; an officer who collects the customs at ferries; a toll-collector; — a guardian at a pass; a road-watch
s.m., or guẕar-gāh, or guẕar-gāh-ě-ʻām, s.f. A passage; a ferry; a ford; a public road, highway, thoroughfare
v.n. To pass; to pass time, &c. ( = guẕrān karnā, q.v.)

def: s.m. = گذاره guẕāra, q.v.
s.m. (see guẕār), s.m. A passing, passing over, crossing; a passage; passing of time or of life; living, subsisting; — stay, abode; — a ferry; ferry-boat; a toll-bar
more: s. m. A passage, a passing; a ferry, a ferryboat.
subs: To effect a passage, to cross over; to get through or across; — to live, to subsist (on, -se); to drag out (one's) days (by means of, -se), to subsist with difficulty
To hit on (or to discover) a means of living

def: n. m. 1. See گزران 2. (H. ghāṭ ) A ferry. 3. A toll bar. 4. Stay; abode. guzāra k. , v. a. To get along with difficulty; to just manage to live. guzāre kā ghāṭ , n. m. A ferry. guzāre kī shakal nikālnā , v. a. To strike out a means for a livelihood. guzāre kī nāo, ghāṭ kī nāo, ghaṭahā . A ferry boat.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گذاره (guzāra).

r: guẕarān, guẕrān, guẕashtan, guẕar, āna, ana
ety: imperf. part. of guẕashtan; and = guẕar, q.v.+Zend āna or ana = S. अन]
def: part. Passing; passing by; passing beyond; — (v.n. of guẕarānīdan), s.f. Passing; passing time or life, living; life; course of life; subsistence; livelihood, employment
more: (from گذريدن) 1. adj. Passing. 2. s. f. Life, living, passing. 3. Employment, livelihood. گذران کرني guẕrān karnī, To pass or spend life or time, to live, subsist.
subs: To pass or spend time, to pass life, to live, to subsist; to contrive to live, to drag out (one's) days.

def: s. m. A ferryman, a guard of a pass or road.
s. f. A passage, ferry, ford, thoroughfare.
s. f. A passage, ferry, ford, thoroughfare.

ety: perf. part. of guẕar-jānā = guẕarnā, q.v.
def: part. adj. (fem. guẕar-gaʼī), Passed away, lapsed; deceased, dead.

ety: guẕār, q.v.+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. Passing; returning; payment, &c. (see guẕār, like which it is used in comp., e.g. shukr-guẕārī, q.v.).

n. f. A market held in the afternoon by the road side. Gudṛī se bīvī āīṅ, 'Shekh jī, kināre ho! ' Prov. Madam's from the market come, 'Shekh for the lady veiled make room!' (She pretends that no strange man has ever seen her face). guzarī, yā gudṛī lagānā , v. a. To lay out things for sale.
s.f. A market held (in the afternoon) by the road-side or in a thoroughfare
more: s. f. A market held in the afternoon by the road side.
subs: To set up a stall, or lay out things for sale, in a road-side market.

def: request
ex: میری آپ سے ایک گزارش ہے۔
merī āp se aik guzāriś hai.
I have a request for you.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گزارش (guzāriš).

def: n. f. 1. Representation; statement (بيان). 2. Request ( ardās , 1). guzārish k. , v. a. See arz k .
s.f. Payment; tribute; representation, explanation; statement; petition, request
more: s. f. 1. Payment. 2. A petition, request. 3. Representation, explanation. گذارش کرنا guẕārish karnā, To represent.
subs: (kī ḵẖidmat-meṅ), To represent (to); to state (syn. ʻarẓ karnā).

def: v.t. To cause to pass; to bring to pass; to pass (life, or time, &c.); to pass by; — to pass or lay before; to exhibit; to state.
more: v. a. To cause to pass, &c., to pass, pass (life).
wety: From Classical Persian گذار (guzār) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

def: v.n. To pass, go, elapse; to come to pass, to happen, to befall; to pass (by or over, -par); to pass (through, par-se, or se); to pass (before, or under, or in review, -se), to be put or laid (before, -se), be presented; to pass (over, -se), to overlook, to omit; to abstain (from), desist (from); to decline; — to pass (beyond), to surpass; to pass away, to die
more: v. n. 1. To pass, to go, to pass by or over, pass time, elapse, surpass, happen, befall. 2. To omit, to abstain from, to decline. 3. To terminate. 4. To die. گذرجانا guẕar-jānā, To pass away, to elapse, to surpass, to outdo.
subs: v.n. (intens.), To pass by, to pass away, to elapse; to die; to move away (from, -se); to pass beyond, to surpass, to outdo
or dar-guẕar karnā (-se), To pass (over), to overlook, to excuse; — to abstain (from), desist (from), to give up; to avoid.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گذر (guzar) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

def: P. H., v. n. See guzrānnā , v. a. and bītnā . 1. To pass; elapse. Khūb guzregī jo mil baiṭheṅge dīvāne do . Prov. When two mad people meet together they chime in well with one another. 2. (Sant. chabāyenā ) To die; expire. Zan bachche rote haiṅ goyā nānī guzar gaī, Hamsāyā pūchhte haiṅ ki 'kyā dādī mar gaī? Bin murde ghar meṅ shor machātī hai muflisī . On poverty. Nazīr. Wife and children wail, as though the grandmother had died to day, Neighbours asking come and go, 'Has your grandsire passed away!' Needs no dead for whom to groan, Where the hungry mourn and moan. guzar-gayā , adj. Deceased; dead. dar-guzarnā, dar guzar k. , v. n. 1. See بازاَتا 2. To overlook; excuse.
P. H. v. a. See pesh k . 1. To bring to pass. 2. To pass the time; to live. 3. To state; exhibit. 4. To lay before. guz'rā , adj. Lapsed; passed away.
passport
wety: Borrowed from Persian گذرنامه (gozarnâme).

def: n. f. 1. Deductions. 2. A grant of land. guzar-bān , n. m. 1. A ferryman. 2. A toll-collector. 3. A guard; watch. guzar-ām, guzargāh-i-ām, guzar-gāh , n. 1. A public road; highway; thoroughfare ( guzārah , 1). haq-i-guzar , n. m. Right of way.

ety: v.n. of guẕāshtan, caus. of guẕashtan; see guẕār
def: s.f. The quitting or leaving; — a deduction (from); — a grant (of land).

ety: perf. part. of guẕashtan; see guẕār
def: part. adj. Past, elapsed; last (year, &c.); left; tendered; — s.m. The past.
more: (from گذشتن) 1. part. pass. Past. 2. s. m. The past. (Plur. گذشتگان).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گذشته.

def: adj. 1. Elapsed; past. Guzastah rā salvāt! Prov. What's past and gone be blessings on! 2. Last; preceding. sāl-i-guzashtah . Last year.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گذشته.

ety: perf. part. of guzīdan; see guzān
def: part. Chosen, selected; preferred; — choice, select; excellent.
more: part. pass. (from گزيدن guzīdan ) Chosen, select, having chosen, &c. ¶ gazīda, part. pass. (from گزيدن gazīdan ) Bitten.

def: adj. To be chosen, worthy of being chosen.
part. act of گزيدن ( In comp .) Adopting; choosing. khilvat-guzīṅ , n. m. One given to retirement; a hermit.
part. adj. Choosing, selecting; electing; preferring; adopting (used as last member of compounds)
more: part. act. (of گزيدن) in compos. Choosing, preferring, electing. خلوت گزين ḵẖalwat-guzīn, Fond of retirement.
subs: adj. & s.m. Making choice of seclusion, fond of retirement or solitude; solitary; retiring from the world; — a recluse, hermit.

def: s.m. (?) Aid, help, remedy (syn. ćāra); — escape
more: n. 1. Escape. 2. Help (P. chārah ). nā-guzīr , adj. Unavoidable; inevitable.
s. Remedy, help. ¶ gazīr or gizīr, s. A person who collects the taxes of a town and pays them to the taḥṣīl-dār, a manager or agent; a commander, a hero, the night watch.

def: furious, full of anger
wety: Derived from Classical Persian غژم (ġužm, “anger, rage”).

def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Passed; passed away, lapsed.

n. f. 1. Living; course of life. Guzar gaī guzrān, kyā jhoṅpṛī kyā maidān . When life is done all's vain, or lowly hut or barren plain. 2. Livelihood ( ājīvkā , 1). Dukkham sukkham guzrān kartī hūṅ . Wom. With toil and sweat I earn my bread. guzrān yā guzar k. , v. a. 1. To subsist; live; pass one's days. 2. To drag on one's days ( din kāṭnā ). Kaise guzar karūṅ rī, sakhiyo! ān paṛī ab to bhārī, Charkhā bodo, māl purānī, dehī būḍh bhaī sārī . Wom. song. Neighbour! I am hard bested. How am I to win my bread? Old my wheel, and old my thread, Old my frame, and hands, and head.
v.t. To pass away time, &c. ( = guẕārnā); to pass before, to put or lay before, to present, offer; to bring forward, to adduce, to produce.
more: v. a. To present, to offer.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گذران (guzarān) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

def: s.m. A cartilage; gristle.
Gwadar (a port city in Balochistan, Pakistan)
wety: Borrowed from Baluchi گوادر (gwādar), a compound of گوات (gwát, “wind, air”) + در (dar, “door, gateway”).

r: gwāl, guʼāl, goʼāl, gwālā, guʼālā, goʼālā
def: s.m. A caste of Hindūs whose occupation it is to attend to cattle; a man of that caste; a cow-keeper, cow-herd; a milkman
subs: or gwālā-bāl, s.m. A cow-herd's child or children

r: gwālā, guʼālā, goʼālā, gwāl, guʼāl, goʼāl
def: s.m. A caste of Hindūs whose occupation it is to attend to cattle; a man of that caste; a cow-keeper, cow-herd; a milkman
more: n. f. S. गोपाल 1. A cowherd. 2. A milkman. go-thān ; Bhoj. gohān , n. m. The place where the cattle of a village are assembled. go-than sītlā , n. f. Cow pox.
subs: or gwālā-bāl, s.m. A cow-herd's child or children

def: s.f. A woman of the gwāl caste; the wife of a gwālā; a cowherdess; a milk-maid.
more: s. f. The wife of a (گوالا) cowherd, a female of the gwāl cast.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Cow-keeping; — a cow-keeper, cow-herd, &c. ( = gwāl).
s.f. = gwālan, q.v. s.f. A woman of the gwāl caste; the wife of a gwālā; a cowherdess; a milk-maid.
s.f. A woman of the gwāl caste; the wife of a gwālā; a cowherdess; a milk-maid.
n. m. Pregnancy ( gābh ).
s.m. = H گيابهن ग्याभन gyābhan, adj. = gābh, and gābhin, qq.v.
adj. = H گيابهہ ग्याभ gyābh, s.m. = gābh, and gābhin, qq.v.
s.m. Knowing, knowledge, understanding, intelligence, apprehension, conception, intellect; — knowledge of a specific and religious kind, which tends to exempt the soul from further transmigration
more: n. m. S. ज्ञान from ज्ञा to know. 1. See بدهـ 1, 2. 2. Special religious knowledge exempting the soul from further transmigration. gyān charchā , n. m. Religious discourse. gyān chau , n. f. Wisdom tooth ( aql dāṛh ). gyān chausar , n. f. A kind of chess. gyān gudṛī, lit . the sage's rags. Friar's rags. 'Bindī mangāī, chhorā, lāyo hai ak nāhīṅ re?' yāhī pe rātoṅ jhagṛī, Nadī kināre lagā dī giyān gudṛī . Rus. Wom. song. 'I sent for spangles, boy, have they been brought?' On this a quarreling all night we got: In friar's rags the river side I've sought! gyān meṅ ānā , v. n. To comprehend. gyān h. , v. n. To get knowledge, esp. religious knowledge. dharm gyān, dharm biddyā, dharm bichār , Moral philosophy.

s. m. 1. Knowledge, understanding, intelligence, intellect, knowing. 2. Knowledge of a specifick and religious kind, which tends to exempt the soul from further transmigration. گيان دوڙانا gyān dauṛānā, To meditate deeply.
subs: (apnā), To exercise the understanding; to meditate deeply
s.f. 'The ragged covering of wisdom or of a sage'; Friar's rags
or gyān-wanṭ, or gyān-wantā, adj. & s.m. Knowing, wise, intelligent, judicious (syn. gyānī); — a wise man, a sage, &c.
(˚na+ind˚), s.m. An organ of perception or of consciousness (e.g. the skin, tongue, eye, ear, mental powers, intellect).
wety: Derived from Sanskrit ज्ञान (jñāna).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Knowing, wise, learned, intelligent, judicious, discreet; — one who understands fully; one who possesses gyān or religious wisdom; — a sage; a philosopher; — a prognosticator; an astrologer; a soothsayer; a fortune-teller.
more: n. m. A sage; one possessing religious knowledge. gy ānī, gyān-vān , adj. See بدهمان
1. adj. Wise, intelligent, judicious, knowing. 2. s. m. A sage, one possessing religious wisdom or گيان gyān, q. v. sign. 2. an astrologer, a fortuneteller.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Knowing, wise, learned, intelligent, judicious, discreet; — one who understands fully; one who possesses gyān or religious wisdom; — a sage; a philosopher; — a prognosticator; an astrologer; a soothsayer; a fortune-teller.

r: hā, hāʼe-hawwaz, h, hawwaz, hāʼe-mudawwara, ha, h., hāʼe-jalī, hāʼe-z̤āhir, hāʼe-malfūz̤ī, bādshāh, hāʼe-mukhtafī, hāʼe-maktūbī, bahāna, ha-kār
ety: called hāʼe-hawwaz, 'h of the word hawwaz,' or hāʼe-mudawwara, 'the round h'
def: s.m. The letter or sound ha.
more: called hā-i-havvaz , and hāë mudavvarah , the 27th letter of the Arabic and 31st of the Persian alphabet, corresponds to ह in Sanskrit. It has the sound of the English h . When it occurs at the end of a word, it is either perceptible, hā-i-zāhir or— jalī , or— malfūzī , as in bādshāh a king; or imperceptible ( makhfī or— maktūbī ), as in parvāna a writ, ba with, etc. where it merely conveys the sound of fatha . When it occurs at the end of Arabic words it is generally with two points over it, thus ة and is pronounced as t . In Abjad it stands for five, and in almanacs it may represent Thursday or Virgo. It is interchangeable as an initial, medial, and final with s , q. v. as hai=sai is. As an initial and medial with g , as W. he = E ge ho! hap=gap gulp; H. hans = Eng. goose; H. harā = Eng. green; hī = Sant. gī very; sahodrā= sagodrā of the same womb: with b , as hai =Bhoj. bā is; Eng. hu…

the twenty-seventh letter of the Arabic and thirty-first of the Persian alphabet, corresponds to ह in Sanskrit. It is one of the guttural letters, having the sound of the English h. When it occurs at the end of a word, it is either perceptible, which is called هاي ظاهريا جلي يا ملفوظي ( hā-i-z̤āhir or -jalī, or -malfūz̤ī ), as in بادشاه bādshāh, A king; or imperceptible مختفي يا مکتوبي ( -muḵẖtafī or -maktūbī ), as in بہانه bahāna, A pretence, به ba, With, &c. where it merely conveys the sound of fatʼḥa. In the formation of the plural with ها the هاي جلي of the singular is preserved; as راهہا rāh-hā, Roads; اندوه ها andoh-hā, Griefs, &c. whereas the هاي مختفي disappears; as, جامہا jāma-hā, Robes. بندها banda-hā, Slaves, &c. def: s.f. At most; at least; extremely, very ( = ḥad-se; az ḥad; e.g. ḥad burā maʻlūm hotā hai)
more: n. f. حد was conterminous with. 1. Boundary; limit; term. 2. A landmark; bar (of a harbour). 3. The utmost point or degree; the maximum ( ant , 1). Had ho gaī . It is the ne plus ultra . 4. A definition. had , adv. At most; at least. Chhai mahīne, had baras roz . For six months, at most a year. had bāṅdhnā, had bandī k., had bast k., had muqarrar k. , v. a. To pass or transgress bounds; to encroach. had javāb , Rejoinder; surrejoinder. hadd-i-samāٴat , n. Limitation statute. hadd-i-samāat se bāhar…
